title: Hear the First Book of HomerвҖҷs Iliad Read Aloud in the Original Greek
description:
in History, Language, Literature |   	June 4th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

You can, of course, learn the Greek lanВӯguage as itвҖҷs spoВӯken today.
body:
in History, Language, Literature |   	June 4th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

You can, of course, learn the Greek lanВӯguage as itвҖҷs spoВӯken today. You can also learn Greek as it was spoВӯken in antiqВӯuiВӯty вҖ” and as it was, until fairВӯly recentВӯly in hisВӯtorВӯiВӯcal time, taught to stuВӯdents in the modВӯern West. But itвҖҷs a fairВӯly difВӯferВӯent endeavВӯor again to learn Greek as Homer spoke it. The fact of the matВӯter is that no human being ever realВӯly spoke like Achilles, AgamemВӯnon, Odysseus, PeneВӯlope, or any of the othВӯer charВӯacВӯters in the IliВӯad andВ Odyssey. HomeВӯrвҖҷs many litВӯerВӯary achieveВӯments through these works include the creВӯation and comВӯmand of a kind of synВӯtheВӯsized poetВӯic Greek, comВӯbinВӯing qualВӯiВӯties of regionВӯal IonВӯic and Aeolic dialects with varВӯiВӯous forms and expresВӯsions that were outВӯdatВӯed even in the eighth cenВӯtuВӯry BC. If it served the meter, Homer used it.
NeedВӯless to say, when most of us attempt to read Homer aloud in the origВӯiВӯnal, we get it all or mostВӯly wrong, even if weвҖҷre familВӯiar with modВӯern Greek. WeвҖҷd have to spend a long time indeed in the world of clasВӯsiВӯcists before hearВӯing a more accuВӯrate recordВӯing than the one above, delivВӯered by a YouTuВӯber called Thomas WhichelВӯlo.
On his chanВӯnel, WhichelВӯlo speВӯcialВӯizes in perВӯformВӯing venВӯerВӯaВӯble litВӯerВӯary texts with a proВӯnunВӯciВӯaВӯtion and cadence as close to periВӯod-accuВӯrate as posВӯsiВӯble, often in the origВӯiВӯnal lanВӯguage, someВӯtimes with his own musiВӯcal accomВӯpaВӯniВӯment. HeвҖҷs done readВӯings of theВ Bible, ShakeВӯspeare, Keats, and Wilde, but none so far has been so popВӯuВӯlar as his renВӯdiВӯtion of the first book of the IliВӯad, accomВӯpaВӯnied by subВӯtiВӯtles of HomeВӯrвҖҷs text and an EngВӯlish transВӯlaВӯtion.
A Greek here in 2026 with no parВӯticВӯuВӯlar knowlВӯedge of the clasВӯsiВӯcal lanВӯguage may underВӯstand a quarВӯter of the indiВӯvidВӯual words WhichelВӯlo uses, and maybe half of them in cerВӯtain pasВӯsages. ActuВӯalВӯly being able to folВӯlow the stoВӯry, howВӯevВӯer, is anothВӯer matВӯter. Still, you can get a surВӯprisВӯing amount out of the video even if you underВӯstand nothВӯing at all, since WhichelВӯlo is aimВӯing not just for linВӯguisВӯtic accuВӯraВӯcy, but also emoВӯtionВӯal resВӯoВӯnance in his delivВӯery. Ignore his glassВӯes, butВӯton-down shirt, microВӯphone, and winВӯdow frame, and you could almost be sitВӯting around a campВӯfire with him nearВӯly 30 cenВӯturies ago. Note, also, that the comВӯmenters include genВӯuine clasВӯsiВӯcists who call his the best readВӯing theyвҖҷve ever heard вҖ” as well as viewВӯers, creВӯdenВӯtialed or othВӯerВӯwise, eager to hear him name all those mighty Achaean ships in Book 2.
RelatВӯed conВӯtent:

Hear What HomerвҖҷs Odyssey SoundВӯed Like When Sung in the OrigВӯiВӯnal Ancient Greek

Hear Beowulf and Gawain and the Green Knight Read in Their OrigВӯiВӯnal Old and MidВӯdle EngВӯlish by an MIT MedievalВӯist
Based in Seoul,В ColВӯinВ MarshallВ writes and broadВӯcasts on cities, lanВӯguage, and culВӯture. HeвҖҷs the author of the newsletВӯterВ Books on CitiesВ as well as the booksВ н•ңкөӯ мҡ”м•Ҫ кёҲм§ҖВ (No SumВӯmaВӯrizВӯing Korea) andВ KoreВӯan Newtro.В FolВӯlow him on the social netВӯwork forВӯmerВӯly known as TwitВӯter atВ @colinmarshall.

title: How Conflict Helped Create Pink FloydвҖҷs вҖңComfortably NumbвҖқ and Its Legendary Guitar Solos
description:
in Music |   	June 3rd, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

Even among the most acclaimed albums ever recordВӯed, not a sinВӯgle one is perВӯfect. That goe
body:
in Music |   	June 3rd, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

Even among the most acclaimed albums ever recordВӯed, not a sinВӯgle one is perВӯfect. That goes more so for the releasВӯes of what I call the вҖңheroВӯic age of the album,вҖқ which enjoyed its zenith around the late sevВӯenВӯties. Not coinВӯciВӯdenВӯtalВӯly, 1979 was the year that Pink Floyd put out The Wall, a rock opera whose sprawl across two discs deals with themes rangВӯing from the bombВӯings of the SecВӯond World War to drug depenВӯdenВӯcy to fasВӯcist impulsВӯes to the isoВӯlaВӯtion of superВӯstarВӯdom. This ambiВӯtion was repaid: The WallВ soon became the best-sellВӯing douВӯble album of all time, despite havВӯing been received with at least a meaВӯsure of ambivaВӯlence over the grandВӯness, or perВӯhaps grandiosВӯiВӯty, of the scale of its proВӯducВӯtion and the tone of its narВӯraВӯtive.
Yet those few preВӯpared to call The Wall an artisВӯtic failВӯure must nevВӯerВӯtheВӯless acknowlВӯedge how much impresВӯsive work it realВӯly does conВӯtain. Of its popВӯuВӯlarВӯly appreВӯciВӯatВӯed achieveВӯments, perВӯhaps the most memВӯoВӯrable is David GilmourвҖҷs guiВӯtar solo, or rather the guiВӯtar solos, on вҖңComВӯfortВӯably Numb,вҖқ a song about being medВӯicalВӯly revived from a subВӯstance-induced stuВӯpor moments before givВӯing a conВӯcert.
They cerВӯtainВӯly stuck in my own head in sevВӯenth grade, when my music teacher assigned our class term paper anaВӯlyzВӯing the album, and kept popВӯping back into it over the subВӯseВӯquent decades. вҖңHis playВӯing is so lyriВӯcal,вҖқ says YouTuВӯber David HartВӯley in his new video about the makВӯing of вҖңComВӯfortВӯably Numb.вҖқ вҖңThe way he plays each note is in a way that you can almost sing it, and the way he uses phrasВӯes is so simВӯple, and so beauВӯtiВӯful.вҖқ
These solos were recordВӯed in a conВӯtext of less-than-smooth sailВӯing for the Floyd: as weвҖҷve preВӯviВӯousВӯly feaВӯtured here on Open CulВӯture, вҖңComВӯfortВӯably NumbвҖқ was the prodВӯuct of anothВӯer arguВӯment puncВӯtuВӯatВӯing the long-frayВӯing partВӯnerВӯship between Gilmour and lead singer Roger Waters, for whom The Wall was a way of renВӯderВӯing his own life expeВӯriВӯences and perВӯcepВӯtions in musiВӯcal form. But as someВӯtimes hapВӯpens, conВӯflict вҖ” in this case, between two comВӯpetВӯing and starkВӯly difВӯferВӯent conВӯcepts of the song, whose evoВӯluВӯtion HartВӯley explains with demo recordВӯings and interВӯview clips вҖ” proВӯduced a greater result than any one artistвҖҷs vision. It all arrives at what HartВӯley calls вҖңposВӯsiВӯbly the greatВӯest guiВӯtar solo of all time,вҖқ which closВӯes out side three, and indeed the most fruitВӯful era of Gilmour and WatersвҖҷ colВӯlabВӯoВӯraВӯtion. Even those who canвҖҷt take The Wall too seriВӯousВӯly have to admit that life isnвҖҷt necВӯesВӯsarВӯiВӯly easy for a rock star, much less for two of them in the same stuВӯdio.
RelatВӯed conВӯtent:
How Pink FloydвҖҷs вҖңComВӯfortВӯably NumbвҖқ Was Born From an ArguВӯment Between Roger Waters & David Gilmour
The HisВӯtoВӯry of the ElecВӯtric GuiВӯtar Solo: A SevВӯen-Part Series
Pink Floyd Songs Played SplenВӯdidВӯly on a Harp GuiВӯtar: вҖңComВӯfortВӯably Numb,вҖқ вҖңWish You Were HereвҖқ & More
Oxford SciВӯenВӯtist Explains the Physics of PlayВӯing ElecВӯtric GuiВӯtar Solos

The EvoВӯluВӯtion of the Rock GuiВӯtar Solo: 28 Solos, SpanВӯning 50 Years, Played in 6 Fun MinВӯutes
Based in Seoul,В ColВӯinВ MarshallВ writes and broadВӯcasts on cities, lanВӯguage, and culВӯture. HeвҖҷs the author of the newsletВӯterВ Books on CitiesВ as well as the booksВ н•ңкөӯ мҡ”м•Ҫ кёҲм§ҖВ (No SumВӯmaВӯrizВӯing Korea) andВ KoreВӯan Newtro.В FolВӯlow him on the social netВӯwork forВӯmerВӯly known as TwitВӯter atВ @colinmarshall.

title: Kurt Vonnegut Diagrams the Shape of All Stories in a MasterвҖҷs Thesis Rejected by U. Chicago
description:
in Design, Literature, Writing |   	June 3rd, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			
вҖңWhat has been my pretВӯtiВӯest conВӯtriВӯbuВӯtion to the culВӯture?вҖқ asked Kurt
body:
in Design, Literature, Writing |   	June 3rd, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			
вҖңWhat has been my pretВӯtiВӯest conВӯtriВӯbuВӯtion to the culВӯture?вҖқ asked Kurt VonВӯnegut in his autoВӯbiВӯogВӯraВӯphy Palm SunВӯday. His answer? His masterвҖҷs theВӯsis in anthroВӯpolВӯoВӯgy for the UniВӯverВӯsiВӯty of ChicaВӯgo, вҖңwhich was rejectВӯed because it was so simВӯple and looked like too much fun.вҖқ The eleВӯgant simВӯplicВӯiВӯty and playВӯfulВӯness of VonnegutвҖҷs idea is exactВӯly its endurВӯing appeal. The idea is so simВӯple, in fact, that VonВӯnegut sums the whole thing up in one eleВӯgant senВӯtence: вҖңThe funВӯdaВӯmenВӯtal idea is that stoВӯries have shapes which can be drawn on graph paper, and that the shape of a givВӯen societyвҖҷs stoВӯries is at least as interВӯestВӯing as the shape of its pots or spearВӯheads.вҖқВ In 2011, we feaВӯtured the video below of VonВӯnegut explainВӯing his theВӯoВӯry, вҖңThe Shapes of StoВӯries.вҖқ We can add to the dry wit of his lesВӯson the picВӯto-infoВӯgraphВӯic by graphВӯic designВӯer Maya EilВӯam above, which strikВӯingВӯly illusВӯtrates, with examВӯples, the varВӯiВӯous stoВӯry shapes VonВӯnegut described in his theВӯsis. (Read a conВӯdensed verВӯsion here.)
The preВӯsenВӯter who introВӯduces VonВӯnegutвҖҷs short lecВӯture tells us that вҖңhis sinВӯguВӯlar view of the world applies not just to his stoВӯries and charВӯacВӯters but to some of his theВӯoВӯries as well.вҖқ This I would affirm. When it comes to puzВӯzling out the import of a stoВӯry IвҖҷve just read, the last perВӯson I usuВӯalВӯly turn to is the author. But when it comes to what ficВӯtion is and does in genВӯerВӯal, I want to hear it from writВӯers of ficВӯtion. Some of the most endurВӯing litВӯerВӯary figВӯures are expert writВӯers on writВӯing. VonВӯnegut, a masВӯter comВӯmuВӯniВӯcaВӯtor, ranks very highВӯly among them. Does it do him a disВӯserВӯvice to conВӯdense his ideas into what look like high-res, low-readВӯabilВӯiВӯty workВӯplace safeВӯty graphВӯics? On the conВӯtrary, I think.


	

Though the design may be a litВӯtle slick for VonВӯnegutвҖҷs unapoloВӯgetВӯiВӯcalВӯly indusВӯtriВӯal approach, heвҖҷd have appreВӯciВӯatВӯed the slightВӯly corny, slightВӯly macabre boilВӯerВӯplate iconogВӯraВӯphy. His work turns a susВӯpiВӯcious eye on overВӯcomВӯpliВӯcatВӯed posВӯturВӯing and chamВӯpiВӯons unsenВӯtiВӯmenВӯtal, MidВӯwestВӯern directВӯness. VonnegutвҖҷs short, trade pubВӯliВӯcaВӯtion essay, вҖңHow to Write With Style,вҖқ is as sucВӯcinct and pracВӯtiВӯcal a stateВӯment on the subВӯject in exisВӯtence. One will encounter no more ruthВӯlessВӯly effiВӯcient list than his вҖңEight Rules for WritВӯing FicВӯtion.вҖқ But itвҖҷs in his вҖңShapes of StoВӯriesвҖқ theВӯoВӯry that I find the most insight into what ficВӯtion does, in brilВӯliantВӯly simВӯple and funВӯny ways that anyВӯone can appreВӯciВӯate.
Note: An earВӯliВӯer verВӯsion of this post appeared on our site in 2014.
RelatВӯed ConВӯtent:В 
Kurt VonВӯnegut Explains вҖңHow to Write With StyleвҖқ
The Shape of A StoВӯry: WritВӯing Tips from Kurt VonВӯnegut
Kurt VonВӯnegut Offers 8 Tips on How to Write Good Short StoВӯries
Kurt VonВӯnegut: Where Do I Get My Ideas From? My DisВӯgust with CivВӯiВӯlizaВӯtion
Josh Jones is a writer and musiВӯcian based in Durham, NC.В

title: Don't Feed the Ducks! A Zany Animation Predicts the Absurd Outcomes of Ignoring the Rules
description: How many people actually heed the warnings about not feeding ducks waddling around public parks? If youвҖҷve taken a flippant approach to these guid
body:
How many people actually heed the warnings about not feeding ducks waddling around public parks? If youвҖҷve taken a flippant approach to these guidelines in the past, we recommend you watch AJ JeffriesвҖҷ new animation, вҖңDUCKS.вҖқ What opens as an innocuous jaunt around a pond quickly turns into a dark comedy full of strange contortions and feathered villains sure to pop into your head the next time you throw a chunk of bread.
Jeffries is also behind this ridiculous story of a struggling horse, and you can find more of his work onВ Instagram,В Vimeo, orВ Behance.

title: Wildfires and War Rage in Shawn Huckins' Theatrical Paintings
description: In Slow Burn, Shawn Huckins puts the cognitive dissonance that defines our current era in stark relief. The New Hampshire-based painter has long c
body:
In Slow Burn, Shawn Huckins puts the cognitive dissonance that defines our current era in stark relief. The New Hampshire-based painter has long challenged American mythology and collective aggrandizing by reinterpreting canonical artworks and visual languages. His series have commented on the U.S.вҖҷs proclivity for erasing history and the ways our garments convey social status and class. In this new body of work, he directs us to the contradictory experience of witnessing destruction as both a spectacle and a distant occurrence. 
Slow Burn presents a suite of landscapes, each veiled by curtains. Floral drapery flanks a catastrophic explosion, a sliver of sunlight peeks through a decorative toile de jouy pattern, and delicate lace veils a wildfire in the distance. Opened just enough to allow a partial glimpse, the curtains suggest a theatrical scene in which the raging disasters of war and the climate crisis are ongoing.
вҖңWar Cloud and Floral Blue CurtainвҖқ (2026), oil and acrylic on canvas, 37 x 30 inches
In contrast to the 19th-century landscapes synonymous with the Hudson River School, Huckins stages scenes in which romanticism and idealized vistas are long gone. While the viewer peers outward from the safety of a home, the fires and bombs that characterize the outside world inch closer, and the complacency, privilege, and pure luck that have provided protection thus far are ever more precarious.
Slow Burn runs from July 11 to August 22 at K Contemporary in Denver. Until then, find more from the artist on Instagram.
вҖңWildfire Sunset Behind Sheer CurtainвҖқ (2026), acrylic on canvas, 60 x 48 inches
вҖңFirst Sun and Green CurtainвҖқ (2026), oil on canvas, 64 x 96 inches
вҖңWildfire Sunrise Behind Floral Lace CurtainвҖқ (2026), acrylic on canvas, 36 x 30 inches
вҖңWar Cloud and Glowing Blue CurtainвҖқ (2026),  oil and acrylic on canvas, 60 x 48 inches
вҖңFirst Sun and Toile de Jouy Curtain (2)вҖқ (2026), oil on canvas, 32 x 26 inches
вҖңSunset and Glowing Green Curtain Study (after Church)вҖқ (2026), oil and acrylic on canvas, 32 x 26 inches

schema.headline: Wildfires and War Rage in Shawn Huckins' Theatrical Paintings

title: From Apocalypse to Renewal, Amy Casey Paints a Surreal World in Distress
description: In Amy CaseyвҖҷs meticulous acrylic paintings, houses and main street buildings whirl through the air amid debris, teeter in huge piles in the sea,
body:
In Amy CaseyвҖҷs meticulous acrylic paintings, houses and main street buildings whirl through the air amid debris, teeter in huge piles in the sea, or balance precariously on giant clusters of fungi. Our perception is tested: are the houses really tiny or are their surroundings exceedingly big? That slippage is at the heart of her practice, which confronts our current, often overwhelming information era and its politics, war, the climate crisis, population displacement, and more. вҖңIt is hard to process the world and the constant flow of information about it without feeling powerless and paralyzed,вҖқ the artist says. вҖңSometimes life just feels like a neverending shriek.вҖқ
In her paintings, which are often as small as six inches wide but can range in size up to several feet, Casey renders actual houses and buildings she has observed mostly around her home in Cleveland. TheyвҖҷre often catapulting through space and losing bits of themselves, which mirrors what Casey describes as witnessing a world вҖңthat seems to become a stranger on a daily basis.вҖқ She adds, вҖңAs I watch a world on the brink, painting it literally falling apart allows me to process life and function on a daily basis,вҖқ she says. вҖңEnvisioning towns in worse-case scenarios serves as an acknowledgment and a hope that there is still time to change; it isnвҖҷt that bad yet.вҖқ
вҖңIn the TreesвҖқ (2025), acrylic on panel, 16 x 16 inches
Recently, Casey has begun to focus on nature, incorporating houses into surreal scenes filled with delicate ink caps or overgrown tree stumps. Even here, the emphasis is on decay, but she tugs on a more hopeful cord to reveal a sense of flourishing and renewal, too. These small paintings revisit houses that have spun away from their previous turmoil and landed in a new resting place, although not without challenges. After all, how does one live on top of an oyster mushroom?
вҖңWe have all experienced our own versions of the world turning upside down and trying to find our footing and start over while feeling small, alone, and overwhelmed by unsettling change,вҖқ the artist says in a statement. вҖңThese paintings meditate on these feelings and what itвҖҷs like to be alive in this overwhelming time and place. These are not gnome or fairy houses in a magical forest. They are the surviving remnants of a world on the precipice, but they are like nature, like us: nothing if not resilient.вҖқ
CaseyвҖҷs work is currently on view in a group show at Brassworks Gallery through June 6 in Portland and Zg Gallery in Chicago. SheвҖҷs also preparing for a show at Maria Neil Art Project this September in Cleveland. Follow updates on CaseyвҖҷs Instagram.
вҖңCrowd SupportвҖқ (2024), acrylic on panel, 6 x 6 inches
вҖңYearningвҖқ (2025), acrylic on panel, 6 x 6 inches
вҖңBreak UpвҖқ (2025), acrylic on panel, 10 x 10 inches
вҖңAvast AmassвҖқ (2025), acrylic on paper, 50 x 60 inches
вҖңPerambulatingвҖқ (2025), acrylic on panel, 24 x 24 inches
вҖңEffulgentвҖқ (2025), acrylic on panel, 6 x 6 inches
вҖңOn a LimbвҖқ (2024), acrylic on panel, 6 x 6 inches
вҖңLuminantвҖқ (2024), acrylic on panel, 6 x 6 inches

title: Jeanne Vicerial's Ethereal Sculptures Dot Historic Spaces in Aix-en-Provence in 'Incarnation'
description: Softness and resilience. Presence and absence. Vitality and stillness. These are just a few of the dualities that permeate the atmospheric work of
body:
Softness and resilience. Presence and absence. Vitality and stillness. These are just a few of the dualities that permeate the atmospheric work of Jeanne Vicerial, whose textile-focused practice taps into history and femininity with precision and reverence.
A city-wide exhibition of VicerialвҖҷs pieces titled Incarnation: Carte blanche Jeanne Vicerial opens across several historic spaces in Aix-en-Provence this month: MusГ©e du Pavillon de VendГҙme, MusГ©e des Tapisseries, Chapelle de laВ Visitation, and MusГ©e Granet. Situated amid centuries-old architecture and existing museum collections, the artistвҖҷs works nod to time, tradition, and remembrance. The show surveys sculptures and installations created throughout the last several years, including sprawling installations that feature yards of pooling rope and smaller, intimate, provocative wall pieces.
вҖңBuste nВ°1 Minerva, вҖҳcelle qui mesure'вҖқ (2024), ropes, threads, bronze,  20 1/2 x 14 1/4 x 8 3/4 inches. Photo В© Laurent Edeline
Known for her dramatic Armors series, Vicerial nods to medieval European heritage, such as knightsвҖҷ armor and burial and memorial customs. вҖңGisante de cЕ“ur,вҖқ for example, which translates to вҖңrecumbent figure of the heart,вҖқ references the tradition of nobility or priestsвҖҷ sculptural likenesses placed atop sarcophagi. Except in VicerialвҖҷs world, these figures are all enigmatically, anonymously, and powerfully female. The silhouettes of bodies are conveyed through textile, which in turn is associated with clothing and its potential to both reveal and conceal as well as protect.
вҖңBeyond practical functions, textiles carry within them a memory: the memory of gestures, of uses, and of the traces left behind by the bodies that have worn or transformed them,вҖқ says a statement. вҖңThus, the material becomes a silent witness to past presencesвҖ”the medium for a history that is simultaneously individual, intimate, and collective.вҖқ
Incarnation opens on June 13 and continues through October 4.
вҖңGisante (Amnios)вҖқ (2022), ropes, threads, and varnished roses, 77 4/7 x 22 3/7 x 13 4/5 inches. Photo В© Adrien Millot
вҖңArmor nВ°5вҖқ (2016-2021) textiles, yarns, cords, knitting, 70 7/8 x 21 5/8 x 17 5/7 inches. Photo В© Laurent Edeline
вҖңSex voto ornГ© nВ°19вҖқ (2025), ropes, copper and brass gilded with fine gold, 9 3/4 x 5 x 3/4 inches. Photo В© Laurent Edeline
